notes: Updated after RSIP article session with attribution and style learnings.

recurring_issues: Missing source attribution in initial research phase
Not requesting access to referenced source material before drafting
successful_fixes: Always request original source material when user references it
Build research brief around actual source data and quotes
Flag when attribution is missing as critical oversight
key_learnings: Thought leadership without proper credit is intellectual appropriation
Direct source material produces stronger, more credible articles than synthetic versions
When user says 'refer to original content' - stop and get that content first

successful_fixes: Tension/reframe structure provides strong narrative backbone
Clear thesis stated upfront maintains focus
key_learnings: Position articles as expanding/applying others' frameworks rather than claiming originality when building on source material

recurring_issues: Vignette openings feel like 'AI technique' that readers spot
Scene-setting apparatus can feel formulaic rather than natural
successful_fixes: Direct conversational openings with shared frustration/recognition
Natural voice with casual language where appropriate
Jump straight into core tension without structural scaffolding
key_learnings: User prefers visceral, dimensionalized style but applied naturally, not through templates
Show-not-tell should emerge from authentic voice, not formulaic 'picture this' scenes
Casual language ('catch their own bullshit') can strengthen authenticity when appropriate

recurring_issues: Attribution depth needs checking even after initial draft
successful_fixes: Breaking dense technical paragraphs improves scannability
Rhythm variation maintains engagement through technical content
key_learnings: Always verify proper attribution is present and prominent
Link back to source material for deeper exploration

successful_fixes: Actionable summary with concrete data points
key_learnings: Include source links in final package for credibility and reader research

critical_lessons: STOP WORKFLOW if user references original source material but it hasn't been accessed yet
Attribution is non-negotiable for thought leadership content
Natural conversational voice > formulaic article structures
User style preferences (visceral, show-not-tell) should inform voice, not create templates
